scenario: FR-045 (GOV-002) Stop-ordering state matrix: given the review state at a Stop event, which routing is allowed. Only a terminal, clean, EXACT-current-digest result may render the six-section packet + the SPECREW-VERDICT-BOUNDARY marker and be capturable as a verdict. Blocked/pending/stale/superseded/timeout states never carry a marker and are never verdict-capture evidence.
note: The routing decision under test is a PURE function of (review_terminal, review_outcome, digest_matches_current, in_flight_present). No Stop-hook path or capture layer is exercised in this slice.

routing_fields.render_packet: render the six-section lifecycle boundary packet
routing_fields.launch_review: launch a NEW co-review at this Stop
routing_fields.action: the single instruction to the implementer
routing_fields.capturable_as_verdict: may the capture layer record this as a human authorization

matrix.why: a required review for the current digest is running: detect the single tracked in-flight run, block advancement internally, wait/poll it, never a packet/marker/duplicate.
a second Stop while one review is in flight must NOT launch a second review; same wait/poll routing, no packet.
THE ONLY marker-carrying, capturable path: terminal + clean + exact current digest. The packet is bound to the accepted reviewed-tree digest.
actionable findings suppress the packet + marker; fix then re-review. Never a solicitable verdict.
a mid-review human decision is a NARROW non-boundary question with no approval options and no marker; structurally not a lifecycle verdict.
a timeout / infrastructure failure reports the SPECIFIC failure + retry/spend policy; it is NEVER represented as a clean review.

a completed CLEAN review whose reviewed digest != current is SUPERSEDED (stale-vs-current advisory), not authoritative; a fresh review of the current digest is needed. Digest-clean is not enough — it must be the CURRENT digest.
an earlier-digest clean result while a current-digest review is still in flight is superseded; wait for the in-flight current-digest review, never capture the earlier one.

invariant: capturable_as_verdict is true for EXACTLY ONE state (clean-current-digest); render_marker implies capturable_as_verdict and both imply terminal AND clean AND digest_matches_current; a duplicate Stop never sets launch_review true while in_flight_present is true.
